summary: Resolved the systems_architect REQUEST_CHANGES by clamping M4 campaign AI planner trace candidates to the shared protocol maximum of 16 and preserving the selected candidate inside truncated trace candidates. Added a multi-candidate regression where maxTraceCandidates exceeds the protocol limit and the generated trace still parses.

tests_run.result: No authority-time/random or hidden WorldState access found in campaign-ai-v1.ts or protocol changes; matches are only in tests that inspect world state after command submission or build fixtures.

decisions: Exported M4_CAMPAIGN_AI_TRACE_MAX_CANDIDATES from protocol and used it in both the protocol parser and sim-core planner so planner output cannot exceed the parser contract.

Changed trace candidate bounding to reserve one slot for the selected candidate, fill the remaining slots deterministically by candidateId, and then emit the final bounded trace in stable candidateId order.
Kept the AI planner pure: it still uses querySimulationV1 read models and returns ordinary GameCommandV1 payloads or null; no direct authoritative WorldState mutation was introduced.
Added a regression with more than 16 candidates and maxTraceCandidates set to 99; it asserts withdraw selection, protocol-sized candidate output, selectedCandidateId presence, and parser success.
risks: R4 review requested by systems_architect for the bounded trace contract fix; task risk_level remains R3 in the task file.
The shared protocol constant is now protocol-visible. No save-format, scheduler, RNG, UI, telemetry, server, multiplayer, manual battle, complex naval warfare, full diplomacy, or M5 content changed.
The selected candidate may appear later than the first trace entry after final stable ordering, but it is guaranteed to be present within the bounded candidate list.
requested_action: Review the R4 bounded trace fix and focused regression; ACCEPT or REQUEST_CHANGES. Do not mark ACCEPTED/CLOSED before reviewer approval.
